sourceMovie.title: Judge Dredd
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Judge Dredd works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Terminator 2: Judgment Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Judge Dredd clicked for you, Terminator 2: Judgment Day (1991) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Judge Dredd clicked for you, Looper (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Judge Dredd?
faq.answer: Try Terminator 2: Judgment Day, Looper, Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets—each shares DNA with Judge Dredd while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Judge Dredd?
faq.answer: Judge Dredd (1995) is best described as Science Fiction, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Judge Dredd worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 5.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
